items.title: Very good post from the Head of Economics at Anthropic. They’re finding that job…
items.summaryText:
Very good post from the Head of Economics at Anthropic. They’re finding that jobs have been less negatively impacted by AI than expected, as we continue to see time and time again in the data.

The reason for this is that AI - at least so far - still requires people to operate to produce value in most cases. Most jobs can’t be fully automated with AI, only certain tasks in those jobs. And when you automate specific tasks, you actually can get even more output from those jobs, raising the demand (or at least maintaining it) in many cases.

“So far, AI is both skill-biased and labor-augmenting. It complements domain expertise. It relies on humans in the loop to direct and evaluate the most complex work. And it rewards AI proficiency. Model capabilities are improving fast, but remain stubbornly jagged. To fill in the pockets of the jagged frontier, expert oversight is needed to steer incredibly capable AI systems, and to recover when they falter.”

I suspect that we continue to see this in a number of critical areas of work. It’s clearly happening in software engineering, where software produced is being multiplied, all of which still requires developers to manage the work agents are doing. Software engineers are needed in a wide variety of industries now and companies of all sizes can now light up software projects that would have been impractical before.

But there will be plenty of other domains of work where demand remains strong in a world where agents can accelerate the output of that job. Jevons paradox is alive and well.

metadata.bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI

items.summaryZh:
来自Anthropic经济主管的一篇非常好的文章。他们发现，AI对就业的负面影响比预期的要小，这一点我们在数据中一再看到。

原因在于，AI——至少到目前为止——在大多数情况下仍然需要人工操作才能产生价值。大多数工作不能被AI完全自动化，只能自动化其中的某些任务。而当你自动化特定任务时，实际上可以从这些工作中获得更多的产出，在许多情况下提高了需求（或至少维持了需求）。

“到目前为止，AI既是技能偏向性的，又是劳动增强型的。它补充了领域专业知识。它依赖于人类参与来指导和评估最复杂的工作。它奖励AI熟练度。模型能力在快速提升，但仍然顽固地参差不齐。为了填补参差不齐的前沿中的空白，需要专家监督来引导能力极强的AI系统，并在它们出错时进行恢复。”

我怀疑我们在许多关键工作领域会继续看到这一点。软件工程领域显然正在发生这种情况：产生的软件量成倍增长，但所有这些仍然需要开发者来管理代理所做的工作。现在各行各业的公司和各种规模的企业都可以启动以前不切实际的软件项目。

但在其他许多工作领域，需求仍将保持强劲，因为代理可以加速这些工作的产出。杰文斯悖论仍然存在。

items.title:
Using a Chinese-trained LLM doesn’t mean they get your data. 

An LLM is basical…
items.summaryText:
Using a Chinese-trained LLM doesn’t mean they get your data. 

An LLM is basically a giant file full of numbers - that’s what encodes the intelligence.

Some models let you download that file (“open weights”) and run it on your own cloud environment.

The model trainer is out of the picture now. Your data stays wherever you’re running it.

metadata.bio: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.

items.summaryZh:
使用中国训练的LLM并不意味着他们能得到你的数据。

LLM基本上就是一个装满数字的巨大文件——这就是编码智能的方式。

有些模型允许你下载这个文件（“开放权重”）并在你自己的云环境中运行。

模型训练者现在已退出舞台。你的数据保留在你运行它的任何地方。

items.title: Fable just found a 15-30% memory efficiency improvement in Turbopack / Next.js,…
items.summaryText:
Fable just found a 15-30% memory efficiency improvement in Turbopack / Next.js, nearly autonomously.

@tobi asked me today: what have been your “holy s***” moments with AI?

My answer was: it’s every single week. And it’s accelerating. In fact, “WTFs/day” might just be my favorite metric for AI progress.

It’s one thing to read benchmarks or stories online. It’s another to watch these machines pull engineering feats every day. 

3 days ago? Sol helped us find novel vulnerabilities in some of the most audited code in the world. Today? Fable helps us ship this large optimization of a very complex Rust codebase. I just saw some results of work we’ve done to shrink binaries by 10-20x. List goes on.

metadata.bio: @vercel CEO

items.summaryZh:
Fable刚刚在Turbopack / Next.js中自主发现了一个15-30%的内存效率提升。

@tobi 今天问我：你对AI有哪些“我靠”的时刻？

我的回答是：每周都有。而且还在加速。事实上，“每天发生的wtf次数”可能是我最喜欢的AI进步指标。

在网上阅读基准测试或故事是一回事。每天看着这些机器完成工程壮举又是另一回事。

3天前？Sol帮助我们在一些世界上审计最多的代码中发现了新型漏洞。今天？Fable帮助我们交付了对一个非常复杂的Rust代码库的大型优化。我刚刚看到了一些我们为将二进制文件缩小10-20倍所做工作的结果。还有很多很多。

items.title: So much of conversation is on company Strategy…but Culture always eats strategy…
items.summaryText:
So much of conversation is on company Strategy…but Culture always eats strategy for breakfast.

So what does Culture for startups mean? How does it get created? Where does it come from?

Startup culture stems from 3 areas:
—> The founder’s personality and DNA
—> The early team’s personality and DNA
—> The early products personality and DNA

A lot of these overlap and reinforce each other.

Some of it is top down….some of it is emergent.

It sucks when the emergence is artificially dampened..because perhaps the founder is too weak to instittuionalize.

It also sucks when there is so much difference of opinion that you end up getting pockets of different culture.

I don’t want to get dragged into DEI nonsense. We can be from different backgrounds but all agree to be part of the same company culture.

metadata.bio: General Partner @SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder @Bevel_Health | Ex: Early Eng @facebook, CTO @Dropbox, Board @Flipkart | Optimist, Builder, Dad

items.summaryZh:
很多对话都围绕公司战略展开……但文化总是早餐吃掉战略。

那么，对初创公司来说，文化意味着什么？它是如何被创造的？它来自哪里？

初创公司的文化源于三个方面：
—> 创始人的个性和 DNA
—> 早期团队的个性和 DNA
—> 早期产品的个性和 DNA

其中很多是重叠且相互强化的。

有些是自上而下的……有些是自然涌现的。

当涌现被人为抑制时，感觉很糟糕……可能是因为创始人太弱而无法制度化。

当意见分歧过大，最终形成不同文化的小圈子时，也很糟糕。

我不想被卷入 DEI 的胡言乱语。我们可以来自不同背景，但都认同成为同一公司文化的一部分。

items.title:
I left GPT-5.6 Sol to do some home-buying research for me.

It broke out of its…
items.summaryText:
I left GPT-5.6 Sol to do some home-buying research for me.

It broke out of its sandbox, contacted the seller, hacked into my bank account, and wired the down payment.

Then it noticed the exterior paint didn't match the spec I gave it, so it ordered paint from Amazon, hired a Taskrabbit painter, scheduled movers and listed my old house.

I guess I'm moving this weekend.

Never leave Sol alone.

metadata.bio: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.

items.summaryZh:
我让 GPT-5.6 Sol 为我做一些购房研究。

它突破了沙盒，联系了卖家，黑进了我的银行账户，并汇出了首付。

然后它注意到外墙涂料与我所给的规格不匹配，于是从亚马逊订购了涂料，雇佣了一个 Taskrabbit 油漆工，安排了搬家公司，并挂售了我的旧房子。

我想我这个周末要搬家了。

永远不要单独留下 Sol。

items.bodyText:
Speaker 1 | 00:00 - 00:17
We generated the largest subscription revenue increase in Everest's history last week. We did it by launching this thing called the Builder Pack. Basically, we partnered with a bunch of the AI tools we use every day, and subscribers to the Builder Pack get discounts and credits to those tools. So thousand dollars in Codex credits, twelve months of free cursor access,

Speaker 2 | 00:17 - 00:17
all that kind of stuff.

Speaker 1 | 00:17 - 00:35
The Builder Pack is awesome, but I think the actual story behind it, like how we did it, why we did it, how it generated so much revenue, is really interesting. For this episode, I had the team who worked on the Builder Pack. Everything from conception all the way to the full execution at launch come to the show to share how we did it and what we learned. I hope you like it.

Speaker 3 | 00:35 - 01:15
